# aibox v0.17.7

Patch release: migration briefing accuracy improvements and version in help header.
No processkit version change — still compatible with v0.8.0.

## Migration briefing improvements

Four accuracy and usability fixes to the migration documents generated by `aibox sync`:

- **Pinned version in snapshot check** — the agent verification checklist now specifies
  the exact versioned snapshot path, e.g. `/workspace/context/templates/processkit/v0.8.0/`
  instead of the generic parent directory

- **Correct processkit version** — the briefing now reads the target processkit version
  from `aibox.toml` rather than from `aibox.lock`. This fixes a bug where the briefing
  showed the old installed version instead of the version sync will install. When a
  processkit upgrade coincides with the aibox upgrade, both versions are shown:
  `v0.8.0 (upgraded from v0.7.0 — aibox.toml now targets v0.8.0)`

- **Multiple pending migrations** — if `aibox sync` was run more than once before
  the agent processed the pending directory (e.g. due to a failed first run), the
  checklist now explicitly states: filenames sort chronologically, the last file is
  authoritative, earlier files may be superseded and should be reviewed and closed

- **Imperative pending check** — the processkit state section no longer says
  "if processkit content changes are pending" (which implies the agent already knows).
  It now instructs the agent to always check `context/migrations/pending/` and act
  based on what it finds

## Version in help header

`aibox --help` now shows the version in the first line:

```
aibox v0.17.7 — manage AI-ready development container environments
```

Resolved at compile time via `concat!` + `env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")`.
